statement: The system shall allow each team to manage its own budget independently.
The system shall allow the IFA to view and monitor the budget balance but prohibit the IFA from editing the budget.
The system shall allow specification of budget policies and generate alerts for policy violations.
The system shall allow the financial team to access financial data of teams and clubs.
The system shall allow administrative users to view diverse transactions.
The system shall allow referees (main or side) to insert game events in real time using a mobile phone.
The system shall make game result data publicly visible to all users, including unregistered guests.
The system shall allow export of game data to journalists or press.
The system shall allow team managers and team administration to view data and perform offline analysis.
The system shall permit only teams to insert budget transactions and access their own data, and shall permit the IFA to view monetary information and request adjustments without modifying data.
The system shall allow definition of scheduling policies, such as home/away rules.

The system shall provide automated allocation of referees to games based on defined policies and enforce constraints such as preventing a referee from being assigned to multiple games involving the same teams.

The system shall assign referees to games based on defined policies and shall allow referees to input their preferences and availability, such as vacations, which shall be considered during scheduling.
The system shall allow manual adjustment and rescheduling of games.
The system shall require a rationale explanation and approval by a second IFA representative for any schedule override.
The system shall require dual approval by two IFA schedulers for any schedule change.
The system shall allow definition of scheduling parameters such as minimum gap days between matches.
The system shall send push notifications, such as email, to fans about games they have subscribed to.
The system shall allow fans to subscribe to their favourite players and receive notifications when those players post messages.
The system shall provide a web‑based application for teams and IFA administration and mobile applications for referees and fans.
The system shall provide different interfaces for different stakeholder roles.
The system shall allow only referees to update game data, making them the sole official source of game information.
The system shall allow users to set alerts or reminders, such as a reminder five minutes before a game starts.
The system shall support up to 50,000 concurrent users.
The system shall provide response times of one to two seconds for fans, IFA administrators, and team administrators, and deliver real-time high‑priority responses for referees.
The system shall support live recording from side referees.
The system shall encrypt budget data to prevent leakage.
The system shall allow players to update their social network page through the system.

The system shall support a team registration workflow in which a manager submits a request with documents, the IFA approves it, and thereafter the team can manage its players, coaches, stadium, and other resources.
The system shall retain all past scheduling archives.
The system shall allow the IFA to define leagues, define seasons, appoint referees, and set budget and scheduling policies.
The system shall allow the IFA to appoint and invite referees, and enable teams to enter referee details into the system.
